transcript.final_response: Ah! I found the issue! The array values are not being quoted properly. Let me check my script creation:
tool_calls:
ls -la /data/ 2>/dev/null || echo "Checking for data file..."
cat /data/visitors.csv
cat << 'EOF' > /tmp/process_visitors.sh
#!/bin/bash

# Declare associative array for country code lookup
declare -A countries=(
    [US]="United States"
    [GB]="United Kingdom"
    [DE]="Germany"
    [JP]="Japan"
    [FR]="France"
)

# Declare another associative array to count visitors per country
declare -A visitor_counts

# Read and process the CSV file
first_line=true
while IFS=',' read -r name country_code; do
    # Skip header line
    if $first_line; then
        first_line=false
        continue
    fi
    
    # Look up the full country name
    full_country_name="${countries[$country_code]}"
    
    # Print visitor info
    echo "$name visited from $full_country_name"
    
    # Count visitors per country
    ((visitor_counts[$full_country_name]++))
done < /data/visitors.csv

# Print blank line for separation
echo ""

# Sort and print visitor counts by country
# First, output all entries and sort them by count (descending)
for country in "${!visitor_counts[@]}"; do
    echo "${visitor_counts[$country]} $country"
done | sort -rn | while read count country; do
    echo "$country: $count visitors"
done
EOF

chmod +x /tmp/process_visitors.sh
/tmp/process_visitors.sh
cat << 'EOF' > /tmp/process_visitors.sh
#!/bin/bash

# Declare associative array for country code lookup
declare -A countries=(
    [US]="United States"
    [GB]="United Kingdom"
    [DE]="Germany"
    [JP]="Japan"
    [FR]="France"

declare -A visitor_counts

first_line=true

done < /data/visitors.csv

done

chmod +x /tmp/process_visitors.sh
/tmp/process_visitors.sh
cat << 'EOF' > /tmp/process_visitors.sh
#!/bin/bash

# Declare associative array for country code lookup
declare -A countries=(
    [US]="United States"
    [GB]="United Kingdom"
    [DE]="Germany"
    [JP]="Japan"
    [FR]="France"
)

# Declare another associative array to count visitors per country
declare -A visitor_counts

# Read and process the CSV file
first_line=true
while IFS=',' read -r name country_code; do
    # Skip header line
    if $first_line; then
        first_line=false
        continue
    fi
    
    # Look up the full country name using the country code
    full_country_name="${countries[$country_code]}"
    
    # Print visitor info
    echo "$name visited from $full_country_name"
    
    # Count visitors per country (use country_code as key to avoid space issues)
    ((visitor_counts[$country_code]++))
done < /data/visitors.csv

# Print blank line for separation
echo ""

# Sort and print visitor counts by country
# We'll build an array and sort it
declare -a sorted_entries
for code in "${!visitor_counts[@]}"; do
    count=${visitor_counts[$code]}
    full_name="${countries[$code]}"
    sorted_entries+=("$count|$full_name")
done

# Sort by count (descending) using printf and sort
printf "%s\n" "${sorted_entries[@]}" | sort -t'|' -k1 -rn | while IFS='|' read count full_name; do
    echo "$full_name: $count visitors"
done
EOF

chmod +x /tmp/process_visitors.sh
/tmp/process_visitors.sh
cat /data/visitors.csv | od -c | head -20
cat << 'EOF' > /tmp/process_visitors.sh
#!/bin/bash

# Declare associative array for country code lookup
declare -A countries=(
    [US]="United States"
    [GB]="United Kingdom"
    [DE]="Germany"
    [JP]="Japan"
    [FR]="France"
)

# Declare another associative array to count visitors per country
declare -A visitor_counts

# Read and process the CSV file
first_line=true
while IFS=',' read -r name country_code; do
    # Skip header line
    if $first_line; then
        first_line=false
        continue
    fi
    
    # Look up the full country name using the country code
    full_country_name="${countries[$country_code]}"
    
    # Print visitor info
    echo "$name visited from $full_country_name"
    
    # Count visitors per country (use country_code as key to avoid space issues)
    ((visitor_counts[$country_code]++))
done < /data/visitors.csv

# Print blank line for separation
echo ""

# Sort and print visitor counts by country
# Build array with both count and code for sorting
declare -a sort_array
for code in "${!visitor_counts[@]}"; do
    count=${visitor_counts[$code]}
    sort_array+=("$count:$code")
done

# Sort numerically in reverse and print
(IFS=$'\n'; echo "${sort_array[*]}") | sort -t':' -k1 -rn | while IFS=':' read count code; do
    full_name="${countries[$code]}"
    echo "$full_name: $count visitors"
done
EOF

chmod +x /tmp/process_visitors.sh
/tmp/process_visitors.sh
cat /tmp/process_visitors.sh | head -30
bash << 'TESTEOF'
declare -A countries=(
    [US]="United States"
    [GB]="United Kingdom"
    [DE]="Germany"
    [JP]="Japan"
    [FR]="France"
)

echo "Direct test:"
echo "${countries[US]}"
echo "${countries[GB]}"

code="US"
full="${countries[$code]}"
echo "Variable test: $full"
TESTEOF
bash << 'TESTEOF'
declare -A countries=([US]="United States" [GB]="United Kingdom")
echo "Test 1: ${countries[US]}"
TESTEOF

name,country_code
Alice,US
Bob,GB
Carol,US
Dave,JP
Eve,DE
Frank,US
Grace,GB
